title: Data Validation with type 'list', the formulae can't be dynamic
author: ahmedfarag1993

comments.author: chrisjcnewton
comments.body: I have the same issue. Did you manage to resolve it?

comments.author: ahmedfarag1993
comments.body:
> I have the same issue. Did you manage to resolve it?

Yes, I solved it by adding the dynamic content to another sheet (on one column) and set the state of  that sheet to "very hidden" so no one can edit or change that hidden sheet or its content. Then, i added the "dataValidations" for the column as follows:
`sheetStudents.dataValidations.add('L2:L9999', {
    type: 'list',
    allowBlank: false,
    formulae: ['=Levels!$A$2:$A$9999'],
    showErrorMessage: true,
    errorStyle: 'error',
    error: 'Please select a valid value from the list',
  });`

Note: 
- **Levels** in this part `formulae: ['=Levels!$A$2:$A$9999']` is the hidden sheet name which contains the dynamic data. Also, **A** is the column name in that sheet.

comments.body:
> @ahmedfarag1993
> format the list into comma separated string as formulaList and then add that in dat validation
> 
> > `"${formulaList}"`

there is a **grave accent** before the starting quotation mark and after the ending quotation mark

comments.body: I want to add a dropdown to a column, but the list array is coming from an API. If the item length goes beyond a particular limit it does not show any data. Does anyone have a workaround for this issue? Can we increase the limit of the dropdown list?

T

> > I have the same issue. Did you manage to resolve it?
> 
> Yes, I solved it by adding the dynamic content to another sheet (on one column) and set the state of that sheet to "very hidden" so no one can edit or change that hidden sheet or its content. Then, i added the "dataValidations" for the column as follows: `sheetStudents.dataValidations.add('L2:L9999', { type: 'list', allowBlank: false, formulae: ['=Levels!$A$2:$A$9999'], showErrorMessage: true, errorStyle: 'error', error: 'Please select a valid value from the list', });`
> 
> Note:
> 
> * **Levels** in this part `formulae: ['=Levels!$A$2:$A$9999']` is the hidden sheet name which contains the dynamic data. Also, **A** is the column name in that sheet.

A good workaround solution for the dynamic list problem.
At first, I was getting a blank cell, after removing the '=' in the formulae field it worked like a charm.
`      worksheet.getCell("B2").dataValidation = {
        type: "list",
        allowBlank: false,
        showErrorMessage: true,
        error: "Please select a supplier",
        formulae: ["Suppliers!$A$2:$A$99"],
      };`
